edges.description: Dissemination produces secondary-stage mucocutaneous and skeletal disease.
Host cellular immunity attempts to clear disseminated treponemes.
Untreated persistent infection can progress to destructive late disease.
Persistent inflammation contributes to chronic destructive lesions.

nodes.description: Secondary bejel reflects disseminated treponemal infection with mucocutaneous lesions and skeletal involvement. Clinical features overlap primary- and secondary-stage T. pallidum subsp. pallidum infection, while endemic treponematoses can destroy tissue and bone.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical perspectives of Treponema pallidum subsp. Endemicum infection in adults, particularly men who have sex with men in the Kansai area, Japan: A case series.
evidence.snippet: The clinical features of cases of TEN infection were similar to those of primary- and secondary-stage T. pallidum subsp. pallidum (TPA) infection.

evidence.reference_title: [Review: endemic treponematoses are not always eradicated].
evidence.snippet: More than 100 million additional children are at risk for these disabling and disfiguring infections which destroy tissue and bone.

nodes.description: Treponemal antigens and opsonized organisms activate macrophage-centered innate and adaptive immune responses. IFN-gamma and TLR2/JAK1/STAT1-linked pathways promote macrophage activation, phagocytosis, and inflammatory cytokine production.

evidence.reference_title: IFNγ Enhances CD64-Potentiated Phagocytosis of Treponema pallidum Opsonized with Human Syphilitic Serum by Human Macrophages.
evidence.snippet: IFNγ-polarized macrophages resulted in a statistically significant increase in opsonophagocytosis of Tp accompanied by enhanced production of cytokines, macrophage activation markers (CD40, CD80), TLRs (TLR2, TLR7, TLR8), chemokines (CCL19, CXCL10, CXCL11), and TH1-promoting cytokines (IL-12, IL-15).

evidence.reference_title: Recombinant protein TprK promotes M1 macrophage polarization and phagocytosis by inducing Indoleamine 2,3-dioxygenase protein production.
evidence.snippet: rTprK induces indoleamine 2,3-dioxygenase (IDO) production through the TLR2/JAK1/STAT1 pathway, which is crucial for modulating macrophage function (P < 0.05).

nodes.description: Penicillin therapy remains the preferred treatment for bejel and other endemic treponematoses.

evidence.snippet: Although penicillin therapy is effective, these infectious diseases have yet to be eliminated.

nodes.description: Untreated persistent bejel can progress to late destructive lesions of the skin, mucosa, nose, palate, and bone, including gangosa-like nasopalatal destruction and skeletal deforming disease.

evidence.reference_title: [Review: endemic treponematoses are not always eradicated].
evidence.snippet: More than 100 million additional children are at risk for these disabling and disfiguring infections which destroy tissue and bone.

nodes.description: Treponema pallidum subsp. endemicum initiates bejel after mucosal or skin contact, producing primary mucosal lesions that may be subtle or clinically resemble primary syphilis in recent adult TEN infections.

evidence.reference_title: Whole genome sequences of Treponema pallidum subsp. endemicum isolated from Cuban patients: The non-clonal character of isolates suggests a persistent human infection rather than a single outbreak.
evidence.snippet: Bejel (endemic syphilis) is a neglected non-venereal disease caused by Treponema pallidum subsp. endemicum (TEN).

evidence.reference_title: Clinical perspectives of Treponema pallidum subsp. Endemicum infection in adults, particularly men who have sex with men in the Kansai area, Japan: A case series.
evidence.snippet: Genital lesions were common as the primary lesion.

nodes.description: Treponema pallidum subsp. endemicum maintains its peptidoglycan cell wall by penicillin-binding protein (PBP) transpeptidase cross-linking. This essential, bacteria-specific reaction is the target of the penicillin used to treat bejel: beta-lactam acylation of the PBP active site halts cross-linking and is treponemicidal, which is why penicillin remains curative with no documented penicillin resistance in the endemic treponematoses.

evidence.snippet: although glycan chain polymerization occurs in the absence of transpeptidation (for example, in the presence of penicillin), efficient transpeptidation requires ongoing GTase reactions
